acceptance_criteria.criterion: The executor accepts callback identity only as explicit command parameters or validated structured state derived from those parameters.
acceptance_criteria.fix_note: Cold-review finding CL-t32e-B-01: test_module_has_no_callback_environment_variable_interface previously asserted an enumerated list of specific env-var-name substrings, which left un-listed env-var names undetected. Replaced with the structurally stronger `assert "os.environ" not in source` / `assert "getenv" not in source`, which the current scripts/coordinator_callback.py already satisfies (zero os.environ/getenv references). No production code change required.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Concurrent/repeated delivery of the same run/event invokes cmux trigger-flash at most once, while distinct intervention and terminal events remain distinguishable.
Malformed refs, missing cmux, stale/mismatched run state, and unwritable state fail visibly without command injection.
acceptance_criteria.fix_note: Phase 5 FINAL_REVIEW fix loop: coordinator promoted two in-loop-reviewer advisories to in-scope AC3 defects. (a) WORKSPACE_REF_RE/SURFACE_REF_RE/RUN_ID_RE/EVENT_NAME_RE used trailing `$` which Python's re treats as end-of-string-or-before-a-trailing-newline, letting e.g. "run-1\n" pass validation; changed all four patterns to `\Z` (strict end-of-string) and added regression tests for trailing LF on workspace/surface refs and event names plus trailing LF and CRLF on run ids. (b) A lock file left behind by a process that crashed between acquiring the O_CREAT|O_EXCL lock and writing the {event}.json state file was silently reported as `already_delivered` without ever invoking cmux. Added `_lock_is_stale()` (age > STALE_LOCK_SECONDS = 30s) so a lock this old with no state file is reclaimed (stale lock removed, acquisition retried once, delivery proceeds normally) instead of masquerading as a legitimate prior delivery; a lock younger than the threshold is left untouched so a genuine concurrent in-flight delivery (existing test_concurrent_repeated_delivery_invokes_cmux_at_most_once) is not disturbed. Added regression tests for both the reclaim path (backdated lock -> delivers, state file written) and the non-reclaim path (fresh lock -> already_delivered, cmux not invoked). Phase 7 Codex adversarial-review REGRESSION fix: `_atomic_write_json` called `tempfile.mkstemp()` OUTSIDE its `try/except OSError`, so an mkstemp() failure at write time (state dir became unwritable/full/removed after the earlier mkdir) leaked a raw traceback instead of the visible StateError every other failure mode raises. Because this write runs only after a successful cmux invocation, that uncaught crash left an orphaned lock with no state file -- exactly the precondition _lock_is_stale treats as reclaimable, risking a second cmux flash for an already delivered event. Moved mkstemp() inside the try block with a guarded `tmp_name`-is-None cleanup (nothing to unlink if mkstemp itself failed) so ANY OSError in the atomic-write sequence, including mkstemp(), re-raises as StateError. Added two regression tests exercising write-time failure distinct from the existing mkdir-failure test: one monkeypatching tempfile.mkstemp to raise OSError (deterministic, root-safe), one making the state directory read-only via a chmod-on-run CommandRunner (real filesystem reproduction, skipped when euid==0).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Focused unit/integration tests cover validation, exactly-once behavior, concurrency, and no-callback behavior.

commits.message: test(CL-t32e): red — coordinator callback executor test suite
feat(CL-t32e): green — shared coordinator callback executor
fix(CL-t32e): address cold-review finding CL-t32e-B-01 (brittle env-var test guard)
fix(CL-t32e): harden ref/run-id/event validation and stale-lock recovery (AC3)

downstream_impacts: Sibling beads CL-gzvu (bin/cld) and CL-eqiq (bin/cdx) can wire their existing --coordinator-workspace/--coordinator-surface prose contract to invoke this executor (e.g. via `uv run python scripts/coordinator_callback.py --run <run_id> --event intervention|terminal [--workspace workspace:<n>] [--surface surface:<n>]`) instead of relying on a best-effort agent-followed prose instruction.
No behavior change to bin/cld or bin/cdx in this bead; both launchers are untouched.
